behaviors_covered: PolicyRequest.__lt__ 4-tier ordering: priority < arrival_time < request_id < id() (vLLM L296-L307)
FCFSRequestQueue: O(1) add/pop/peek/prepend, FIFO order, iter without consume
PriorityRequestQueue: heap order by (priority, arrival), peek/pop empty raises, iter on copy
K18 invariant: PriorityRequestQueue.prepend_request == add_request (NO front concept)
create_request_queue factory dispatches by SchedulingPolicy enum
Determinism: re-pushing same items in any permutation gives same pop order
select_preemption_victim FCFS: returns running[-1] (tail / last-admitted)
K19: select_preemption_victim PRIORITY uses max(priority, arrival_time) — worst-priority running
Priority tie-break in victim selection: latest arrival wins among tied priorities
K20: select_waiting_queue FCFS prefers skipped unconditionally (vLLM L1568-L1569)
select_waiting_queue PRIORITY peeks both heads, returns better-head queue
effective_token_budget: PAUSED_ALL → 0, PAUSED_NEW unchanged, UNPAUSED unchanged
PreemptionStrategy enum has all three (recompute / swap / abort) for analytical comparison
P02 length-independence: crossover_prompt_length(short) == crossover_prompt_length(long) for same model shape
KV bytes formula: 2 * NL * NH * D * dt * L; 8K/32/8/128/fp16 = 1 GiB exact
Demo §3 table reproduces (recompute/swap/abort latencies for 512/2K/8K/32K)
E[latency] under 5% OOM/100 steps: 983ms recompute, 476ms swap, 25164ms abort
WorkloadProfile head-of-line blocking factor = 8.00x for demo §2 workload
has_starvation: FCFS True (factor>1), PRIORITY False (well-assigned)
priority_ordering: demo §4 [A,B,C,D] arrival → [B,D,C,A] priority order
aged_priority: demo §4 A's effective priority at t=10 = -7 (NOT in vLLM, pedagogical)
EngineConfig + estimate_p95_ttft: 800ms (max_seqs=8/B=512) → 50ms (max_seqs=128/B=8192) = 16x improvement
long_prefill_token_threshold effect: with threshold=512 at B=2048, short fits in leftover → 50ms
pareto_front: dominance check on (throughput, p95_ttft); demo §5 yields exactly 1 dominant point
End-to-end demo replay (all 5 sections)
Cross-chapter regression: Ch04 scheduler.py and Ch05 block_pool.py both importable in same session

wisdom_applied: W02 (preemption-test design): not direct (Ch06 has no live preemption flow), but principle 'don't pass for the wrong reason' is honored — every demo number is asserted exactly, not 'positive' or 'in range'.
knowledge_applied: K19 (PRIORITY victim uses max not min): test_priority_picks_worst_priority_value + test_K19_priority_victim_uses_max_not_min
K20 (skipped wins under FCFS): test_fcfs_prefers_skipped + test_K20_skipped_wins_under_fcfs
P01 (only RECOMPUTE is preemption path): documented in module docstring; test_P01 ensures enum has all 3 for analytical purposes only
P03 (long_prefill_token_threshold gives 4x p95 TTFT): test_sixteen_x_p95_ttft_improvement (the demo's stronger 16x claim, with threshold=512 + max_seqs/B sweep)
reference_count_observation: Implementer reported 60 # REFERENCE: comments — exactly at baseline floor (Ch04=65, Ch05=61). Each test mapping back to a vLLM line is solid; no sparse-coverage modules detected. Pareto module's REFERENCE coverage is the lightest (~5 references in ~167 lines), justified because pareto.py is analytical synthesis, not a 1:1 port. Not flagged as a fidelity concern.
